name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-103
title: tdd-step.py deployed copy stale relative to source (post Wave 6)
description:
The source script .claude/features/tdd-state-machine/scripts/tdd-step.py (SHA256 86a6c8d6b7870893ed2b37f73305391f2090335ad4b7348cc1d6fb35e1046d4d) does NOT match the deployed copy .claude/agents/tdd-subagent/scripts/tdd-step.py (SHA256 d12a6adcf2dc084dc197f23d2558762b3097c10eec5c00f2d66a72c0f5e56e06).

Source last updated by 0e14043f ("fix(tdd-state-machine): simplify tdd-step.py per Wave 6 audit (bump to 1.4.0)"); deployed copy last rebuilt by 98cd6952 ("build: rebuild tdd-step.py deployed copy (post BUG-6 cleanup)"). So the Wave 6 simplification was never re-deployed.

Detected during Plan E.tdd-state-machine migration verification (gate: 'running the manifest produces 0 working-tree changes'). The new meta-contract manifest in tdd-state-machine/feature.json would modify .claude/agents/tdd-subagent/scripts/tdd-step.py if run against the workspace. The deploy-parity e2e test runs in a tempdir and passes byte-identically, but the real workspace drift is pre-existing.

Impact: tdd-subagent agents execute an older tdd-step.py than the source-of-truth.

Fix: run rabbit-cage's install.py (or the equivalent publish mechanism) to rebuild .claude/agents/tdd-subagent/scripts/tdd-step.py from current source. Once Plan F retires publish.json, the new manifest in tdd-state-machine/feature.json will be the deploy mechanism.

Out of scope for Plan E.tdd-state-machine wave (would require writing into .claude/agents/tdd-subagent/, an anti-scope per PROMPT.md). Filed per B/B PROTOCOL.

history.note: No longer reproducible: source and deployed tdd-step.py now share identical SHA256 (verified in ws42 at HEAD a51da72a). The drift detected during ws66 Plan E.tdd-state-machine was fixed by subsequent publish-loop runs across the meta-contract integration. No code change needed; marking moot before main merge.
